english: gyúr-nas (gyur nas) when they had precious stones enough Dzl.; °di-tsam-gyis čóg-pa ('di tsam gyis chog pa) ma yin-no (ma yin no) that is not enough, that will not do Dzl.; sgál-pa mi brgya žon čóg-pa (sgal pa mi brgya zhon chog pa) his back (is) large enough for a hundred men to ride on it Glr.; adv.: čóg-par (chog par) sufficiently, e.g. sbyin-pa (sbyin pa) to give Dzl.; *ma čóg-pa* (*ma chog pa*) or *-ga* (*-ga*) W. (col. for čóg-par (chog par)), *duṅ - če ma čog - ga sad soṅ* (*dung che ma chog ga sad song*) he not only struck but killed him; p̌yín-pas čóg-gi (phyin pas chog gi) it being sufficient (for the present) that I have come Mil.; tams-cád-la čóg-par gyúr-te (thams cad la chog par gyur te) as all were satisfied Dzl.; čóg-par °dzin-pa (chog par 'dzin pa) to deem a thing sufficient, to be contented or satisfied with it; čog śes-pa (chog shes pa) vb., sbst., adj. to be contented, contentment, content; ltá-bas čog mi śes-pai rdzas (lta bas chog mi shes pai rdzas) a thing at which one cannot look enough Glr., Pth.; yo-byád-kyi (yo byad kyi) (better kyis (kyis)) čog śes-pa (chog shes pa) easily satisfied as to the necessaries of life. — 2. to be allowed, permitted, at liberty, construed in the same manner: k'rid-pas čog (khrid pas chog) you may have lessons with me, I will instruct you Mil.; grán-pas čog (gran pas chog) I am quite at liberty to compete with you, we may safely compete with each other Glr.; ts'ó-ba dráṅs-pas čog (tsho ba drangs pas chog) you can have meat set before you Mil.; with a root: bu byin čog (bu byin chog) then you may render up your son; hence it is in W. the usual word for rún-ba (rung ba), *náṅ-du ča čóg-če yin-na man* (*nang du cha chog che yin na man*) is it allowed to enter or not? *srád-ma za čog* (*srad ma za chog*) eating pease is allowed, also: pease are edible; *lé-na kyoṅ čog ka taṅ* (*le na khyong chog ka tang*) he issued an edict, that it should be permitted to fetch wool, i.e. he (the Maharajah of Kashmir) permitted the export of wool; *léb-na p̌ul čog* (*leb na phul chog*) when it arrives, I shall take the liberty of sending it to you.
seldom for °čág-pa ('chag pa) to be broken Mil.
a transparent, variegated, half-precious stone brought from India to Ld. and considered less valuable than yzi (gzi); perh. cornelian sardonyx?

1. C. the cutting off; deciding; *tal-čó gya čém-po jhé-pa* (*tal cho gya chem po jhe pa*) to bring about a great remission of taxes, *bhu-lọn-čó* (*bhu lon cho*) re-mission of debts; *sa-čó gya čém-po jhé-pa* (*sa cho gya chem po jhe pa*) to make a great way; cf. however p̌yod (phyod). — 2. partition-wall Sch., čod rgyág-pa (chod rgyag pa) prob. to construct a partition-wall. — 3. v. gcód-pa (gcod pa).

1. to be cut off, lám-sgo gnyis ká-bas čód-de (lam sgo gnyis ka bas chod de) both approaches being cut off or obstructed by snow Mil.; bcad kyaṅ mi čód-do (bcad kyang mi chod do) impossible to be severed, caedendo non caeduntur, Glr.; mi-čód-rdó-rje (mi chod rdo rje) a diamond that cannot be cut to pieces, an epithet of a firm unbending king Pth. — 2. to be decided, settled, fixed, goṅ-táṅ dpyád-kyis (gong thang dpyad kyis) (or pas (pas)) mi čod (mi chod) Glr. the value (of the stone) cannot be fixed, though one should attempt to apprize it i.e. it is invaluable, priceless; go čód-pa (go chod pa) v. go. (go).
W. 1. split, cut through; 2. distinct, of words or writings.

1. W. (cog. to čud? (chud)) useless, to no purpose, rin čon soṅ (rin chon song) the payment has been useless, thrown away; gen. adv. *čón-la* (*chon la*) gratuitously, in vain, for nothing, *čón-la k'ón-če* (*chon la khon che*) to hate without cause or reason; *čón-la dád-če* (*chon la dad che*) to sit idle, to spend one's time unprofitably. — 2. tent (?), čoṅ-tág (chong thag) tent-rope Mil., čoṅ-p̌úr (chong phur) tent-pin.

1. robbery, čóms-kyis zas ts'ól-ba (choms kyis zas tshol ba) to live on robbery Ma.; čóm-po (chom po) robber Dzl., čóm-po rkún-ma (chom po rkun ma) robber and thief, gen. čom-rkún (chom rkun), čom-rkún-gyi jigs-pa (chom rkun gyi 'jigs pa) fear of robbers and thieves; čom-rkún-pa (chom rkun pa) id. Stg. — 2. imp. of jóms-pa ('joms pa).
to be finished, accomplished, W., *tó-re čom yin* (*tho re chom yin*) to-morrow it will be finished, *da čom soṅ* (*da chom song*) now it is done, completed; cf. čam (cham).

1. inconstant Cs.; dpyid-čol (dpyid chol) fickle spring-weather. — 2. Cs.: for čó-lo (cho lo) in compounds, rus-čól (rus chol) a die made of bone; śiṅ-čól (shing chol) a wooden die; duṅ-čól (dung chol) shells used inst. of dice(?).
Sch.: 'a hole made by a blow; a nest'.
